text: fat is 21g. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for meats, fat quality matters a lot once the protein is already there.
carbs is 0g. everything else here is pretty flat. in this category, carbs barely matter.
protein is 25g. protein quality is a real strength here. strong protein is expected here, so the rest of the profile decides how high it climbs.
vitamin b12 at 88% DV, vitamin d at 0% DV stand out most. that gives this food real vitamin support.
zinc at 30% DV, iron at 11% DV, potassium at 7% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

the upsides first. high satiety protein source because that is a major real-world strength for meat. complete protein delivery is reliable because the category is supposed to do this well. easy to centre a meal around because practical meal value matters.

the drawbacks next. fat quality can go sideways fast because some meats are strong until the fat profile shows up. cost and storage can be a pain because practical downsides still count. brings no fibre support because that leaves a nutrition gap the rest of the meal has to cover.
B tier. Best when you want efficient protein and can accept the category tradeoffs that come with it.
